title: Review: nie-wdw shielded tx builder + error/happy-path tests
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one P0 (migration atomicity, process-kill window creates permanently broken wallet) and two P1 (cast pattern inconsistency, unsafe coupled to internal step numbering). The P0 is real but narrow window; the unsafe is correctly documented but fragile to future refactors of build_shielded_tx.
Confidence: 12% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) the Groth16 proof path is untested without real params — the happy-path tests silently skip, so proof correctness is not validated here; (2) the scanner-side (how note_diversifier/note_pk_d/note_rseed actually get written to the DB) is not in this diff and was not reviewed; if the scanner writes wrong column types or byte orders, spendable_note_to_sapling will reject them at runtime rather than test time. Would raise confidence if: (a) params were available and happy-path tests actually ran, (b) scanner code that writes the plaintext columns was reviewed alongside this diff.
